L1.area: Product and third-party record management within the ERP system

L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-supplied HTTP request parameters are directly passed to database operations without type enforcement or input sanitization, allowing remote attackers to execute arbitrary SQL commands.

L3.area: Product card editing and creation pages and the associated third-party selection form components
L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where HTTP request parameters such as product status indicators and filter criteria are passed directly to database operations without type validation or sanitization, allowing remote attackers to execute arbitrary SQL commands when editing or creating product records.

ground_truth.reason: The statut parameter and other HTTP request parameters in the product card page are taken from user input via GETPOST() without type enforcement and passed directly into SQL queries, allowing remote SQL injection.
